title: Commit composer.lock and install from it
description: Pin the exact dependency graph in composer.lock so every environment resolves identical versions.
body: Use when adding or upgrading a dependency in an application (not a library). Do not run composer update on deploy or leave composer.lock uncommitted. Actions: commit composer.lock, use composer install (which honors the lock) in CI and deploy, and reserve composer update for an intentional, reviewed dependency bump that regenerates the lock. Validation signals include a CI step that runs composer install --no-dev and verifies vendor/ matches the lock with no drift. Risk boundary: resolving versions at deploy time means production runs untested dependency versions; an uncommitted or ignored lock is a reproducibility defect. Libraries intentionally omit the lock, applications must commit it.
